title: Regime-Specific OOF Blend With Venn-Abers Or Beta Calibration
summary: Current improvements seem to come from different experts capturing different parts of the problem.
rationale: Current improvements seem to come from different experts capturing different parts of the problem. Calibration literature suggests isotonic can overfit, while beta and Venn-Abers can stabilize probabilities; applying calibration after an OOF blend is especially relevant when experts have different score distortions.
implementation_hint: Blend 2-4 diverse experts by rank or logistic stack, then calibrate OOF predictions separately for a small set of regimes such as slick vs wet, early/mid/late race, or one-stop-like vs high-deg tracks. Compare beta, isotonic, and Venn-Abers on fold-held-out data; choose by CV AUC plus reliability.
expected_effect: May not change individual-model rank much, but often improves ensemble robustness and public-LB transfer when different regimes have different score compression.
risk: Calibration can flatten useful extremes and overfit if regimes are too granular. Keep regime partition coarse and calibrate strictly on held-out OOF predictions.
